name: First State Bank
description_short: First State Bank New London, WI — First State Bank is a Wisconsin-based community bank offering checking accounts, agricultural financing, mobile bankin...
description_long: First State Bank operates as a community-focused financial institution based in New London, Wisconsin, with FDIC insurance backing. The bank emphasizes accessibility and user-friendly digital banking experiences alongside traditional in-branch services. Their product offerings include personal checking accounts, agricultural lines of credit for farming businesses, mobile banking with integrated payment processing, and comprehensive fraud prevention resources. The institution distinguishes itself through community engagement initiatives, including annual scholarship programs for graduating seniors ($1,000 awards), volunteerism commitments, and career opportunities within the organization. First State Bank provides modern banking conveniences such as a downloadable mobile app, online account enrollment, password recovery services, and multi-channel access through branches and ATMs. The bank actively educates customers about fraud threats, including check washing scams and card theft, with dedicated fraud alert resources and secure card reporting processes. For consumers seeking straightforward banking with local community roots and agricultural financing options, First State Bank represents a regional alternative to larger national institutions. However, as a smaller regional bank, their product breadth may be more limited than mega-banks, and their service area appears concentrated to Wisconsin. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts with online access
Agricultural lines of credit for farming operations
Mobile banking app with Tap2Local payment processing
Online account enrollment and password recovery
Fraud alert notifications and scam prevention education
Secure online contact forms for customer inquiries
User guides and reference materials for online banking
Annual scholarship program for graduating seniors
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Integrated Tap2Local payment solution in mobile app—no extra hardware required for business payments
Dedicated agricultural lines of credit designed specifically for farming businesses
Active community giving programs including $1,000 annual scholarships for graduating seniors
Comprehensive fraud alert and education resources with real-time scam notifications
Modern mobile banking app with online enrollment and account management
Local branch and ATM network with personalized service available
cons: Limited geographic presence—appears to operate only in Wisconsin, restricting accessibility for out-of-state customers
Website content does not detail specific checking account features, fee structures, or APY rates
No mention of savings accounts, CDs, investment products, or wealth management services beyond basic banking
Small regional bank may have fewer digital features or customer service hours compared to national competitors

contact.address: 220 W Waupaca St, New London, WI 54961

best_for: Wisconsin-based farming and agricultural businesses seeking specialized lending solutions
Community-oriented consumers seeking local banking with FDIC protection and personal service
Small business owners needing integrated payment processing through mobile banking
Graduating high school seniors in the bank's service area eligible for annual scholarships
diagnosis: First State Bank is best for Wisconsin residents and agricultural businesses seeking community-focused banking with FDIC protection, agricultural financing, and modern mobile features. The main caveat is its limited geographic footprint (Wisconsin only) and lack of detailed information about account features, fees, and expanded product offerings beyond basic checking and business lending.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 220 W Waupaca St, New London, WI 54961
